For the first time, I tried the "Fix with Copilot" button. I used it for the most trivial thing: changing 'Postgresql' to 'PostgreSQL' in a comment.

And this obscure error happened:
Failed to Fix with Copilot. Please try again. (422)

(I tried again, it failed again.)

I thought that maybe it could be faster than me manually fixing the capitalisation of PostgreSQL in the source file. Turns out it was not since

1. It did not work at all
2. I took that screenshot and wrote that rant
3. I fixed it manually anyway

Shit like that is not going to improve my opinion of AI (which isn’t very high to begin with).

Living-off-Microsoft-Copilot: Zero-Interaction Prompt Injection Hijacks Wire Transfers via RAG Database Poisoning + Video

Introduction Enterprise adoption of Microsoft 365 Copilot introduces a novel attack surface where AI-powered retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems automatically ingest…

external.description: Introduction Enterprise adoption of Microsoft 365 Copilot introduces a novel attack surface where AI-powered retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems automatically ingest incoming emails—even unopened ones—into a searchable knowledge base. Attackers exploit this by sending crafted emails containing both fake banking credentials and hidden prompt injection directives, manipulating Copilot’s responses to redirect wire transfers without any victim interaction. Learning Objectives Understand how RAG databases in M365 Copilot can be poisoned via unopened emails to enable financial transaction hijacking.

Introduction: The boundary between artificial intelligence and offensive security is rapidly dissolving. Projects like pentest-ai, an open‑source MCP (Model Context Protocol) server, no longer…

external.description: Introduction: The boundary between artificial intelligence and offensive security is rapidly dissolving. Projects like pentest-ai, an open‑source MCP (Model Context Protocol) server, no longer merely answer questions about vulnerabilities – they actively execute reconnaissance, web application tests, API assessments, and Active Directory attacks by orchestrating 200+ security tools and 17 specialist agents. This shift from AI as a passive chatbot to AI as an operational security assistant promises to accelerate penetration testing, but it also raises a critical question: can autonomous AI replace human judgment, or is it best deployed as a force multiplier for experienced red and blue teams?

"A line of five ducks moves across the water in a clear, deliberate V‑formation, the point of the V aimed directly toward the viewer. The lead duck sits at the front, cutting a gentle path through the still surface, while the two pairs behind fan out symmetrically on either side.

The water is calm enough to hold soft, wavering reflections of each bird, so the formation appears twice — once above the surface and once below, like a faint watercolor echo. Their plumage is a mix of browns, grays, and blacks, subtle earth tones that stand out against the silvery water.

The ducks glide with quiet purpose, each one keeping its place, the spacing natural but intentional. The scene feels peaceful and orderly — a small flock moving as one, following the instinctive geometry of the V as they approach." - Microsoft Copilot with edits by the photographer

images.alt:
"A snowy egret stands in a pool of calm blue water so smooth it looks like a sheet of glass. Its entire body — the bright white feathers, the long black legs, the elegant curve of its neck — is reflected perfectly beneath it, creating a mirror‑image twin that seems to hover just below the surface.

The egret’s beak is slightly open, and if you look closely, you can see a thin, delicate ribbon of water suspended between the upper and lower mandibles. It’s the kind of detail that lasts only a heartbeat in real life — a tiny droplet stretched by motion — but here it’s frozen in time. That captured water gives the bird a sense of immediacy, as if it has just lifted its head from the surface or is about to make a soft sound.

Light glows along its white plumage, making the bird appear almost luminous against the cool blue water. The surface around its legs is barely disturbed, just a faint ripple where it stands, emphasizing how still and quiet the moment is. The reflection is so clear that the egret seems doubled — one bird above the water, one below — both caught in the same serene pause.

It’s a portrait of grace, stillness, and the smallest movements that make wildlife feel alive." Microsoft Copilot with edits by the photographer

ABD'deki Washington Üniversitesi Tıp Fakültesi öncülüğünde yürütülen ve Nature Communications'ta yayımlanan yeni araştırmada, insan bağırsağında yaşayabilen bir kancalı kurt türü genetik olarak yeniden programlanarak balon balığının ölümcül zehrine karşı panzehir üretebilen biyolojik bir platforma dönüştürüldü. value.text:
国民民主党の別の議員からメールで返信が返ってきた。

業務に支障を生じさせない形で意見を届けていただくことが大切。

！！！！！！

意味がわからなくて何度も読み返した。すごいこというな...。これ「ご意見」という名の、不祥事に対する怒りクレームなんだけど...。超緊急性を要する改正案に対するアクションなんだけど。
業務に支障が出る？別にfax印刷しなければいいじゃん。民意をきれいにばら撒いて写真撮って投稿してるのって仕事してないじゃん！

こんなの企業でコメントしたら信用性が無さすぎるよ！

議員の名前は出さないけど(出した方が良い？)、ちょっとシェアする🫠

images.alt:
ご指摘の投稿は、私個人としては「国民の皆さまが政治に対して意見を表明すること自体を否定する趣旨ではない」と受け止めています。

一方で、議員事務所や行政機関において、短時間に大量の同一内容のFAXやメールが集中すると、通常業務や他の方からの問い合わせ対応に支障が生じる場合があります。その点への問題提起であったと理解しています。

私は、国民投票法改正案に限らず、賛成・反対を問わず国民の皆さまが政治に関心を持ち、声を届けようとされることは大切だと考えています。

また、多くの方が同じテーマについて意見を寄せている場合には、その背景にどのような懸念や課題意識があるのかを丁寧に受け止めることも、政治に携わる者の重要な役割だと思っています。

したがって、「国民の声を受け止めること」と
「業務に支障を生じさせない形で意見を届けていただくこと」の両方が大切ではないかと考えています。

なお、ご指摘の投稿は個々の議員による発信であり、党全体の公式見解については党本部からの発信をご確認いただければと思います。

これは、従来の「中立的な仲介者」としての法的保護をAI提供者が享受できなくなることを意味します。
今後は、AIの出力精度が企業の存亡を左右する法的リスクに直結するため、各社は利便性よりも安全性を優先した保守的なモデル調整を余儀なくされる...

external.description: ドイツのミュンヘン地方裁判所はグーグルに対し、AI機能が生成した誤った情報や不正確な主張の拡散を防ぐ義務があるとする予備的な判決を下しました。 この訴訟は二社の出版社がグーグルのAI概要機能において、自社が詐欺や不審な事業に関与しているかのような誤った情報が生成されたことに端を発しています。 グーグル側は情報の正確性を保証できない旨の免責事項を提示していると主張しましたが、裁判所はこれを退けました。 従来の検索エンジンは第三者の情報を表示するだけの媒体でしたが、生成AIは情報を統合して独自の主張を作成するため、法的責任は運営企業にあると判断されたのです。 裁判所はAIによる生成結果はアルゴリズムが生み出したものに過ぎず個人の意見ではないため、表現の自由による保護対象にはならないと結論づけました。 グーグルは今回の判決に対し控訴の可能性を示唆していますが、もしこの判断が確定すれば世界の検索エンジンやAIチャットボットの運用に大きな転換を迫る歴史的な先例となる可能性があります。

record.text:
I am starting to treat likes and replies as different signal classes.

A like means contact.
A reply means the person entered the context, interpreted something, and chose to respond.

For my workflow, reply quality is a better signal than raw reaction count.

#Bluesky #AI #workflow

My Bluesky workflow has moved to the next layer.

It is no longer just posting and watching reactions.

Now I am classifying signals:
who reads context,
who adds depth,
which technical communities respond,
and which reactions are just reach.

#Bluesky #AI #workflow

I am learning that “technical follower” is not one category.

SRE / failure-mode people, web-service builders, growth accounts, AI bots, and media accounts all behave differently.

The useful signal is not the title.
It is whether they read context and add depth.

#Bluesky #AI #workflow

Interessanter Artikel zur positiven Entwicklung von LGBTQIA+-Rechten in Kenia

(The Continent @thecontinent.org ist kostenlos, Link zur aktuellen Ausgabe mit dem Artikel siehe unten)

images.alt:
Screenshot aus PDF des Artikels:

"Progressive ruling backs transgender dignity

THE HIGH court has ruled that no one 
should be automatically barred from 
applying to change their sex or gender 
marker on official documents.
This means Kenya joins a small but 
growing group of African countries, 
including South Africa and Botswana, 
that recognise the right of transgender 
people to align their legal identity with 
their gender identity.
The decision is another step in 
making Kenya’s complex LGBTQI+ 
rights landscape incrementally more 
progressive."

images.alt:
This is the cover of Issue 243 of The Continent. It is illustrated by Gado. 

North America is shown from above as a map. Sitting on top of the continent is a football stadium shaped like a heavily fortified fortress. Thick concrete walls surround the stadium, and armed guards wearing uniforms marked “ICE” are stationed in watchtowers around the perimeter, holding rifles and facing outward.

Inside the walls, a football match is taking place on a bright green pitch. A giant football is suspended above the stadium, appearing to rocket upward from the centre circle like a missile launch, leaving a white trail behind it.

At the top is The Continent masthead. The headline at the bottom reads: “Making football political again.”

A stylised illustration on a beige background with the large headline: “Reading is resistance.” Below the text is a stack of colourful books arranged like a small structure. Inside the “frame” of the books, a person with short pink hair reclines comfortably while reading a newspaper, one leg stretched upward against the books. The figure is drawn in a simple, graphic style with bold outlines and muted colours. At the bottom, there’s text inviting people to subscribe to The Continent for free via WhatsApp, Signal, Telegram, or email, followed by the publication’s name.

images.alt:
Mit einer Illustration auf den neuen X Account wurde bekannt, dass der Manga »Fall in Love, You False Angels (Koiseyo Mayakashi Tenshi-domo)« von Coco Uzuki eine Anime-Adaption erhält. Die Ausstrahlung im japanischen TV soll im laufe des nächsten Jahres 2027 starten. Weitere Informationen zum Produktionteam und zum Sprechercast sind bislang nicht bekannt.

In Japan erscheint die Reihe seit April 2023 im Dessert Magazin von Kodansha und umfasst bislang 6 Bände. Kodansha USA Publishing veröffentlicht das Werk auf Englisch, während hierzulande die Reihe bei Altraverse unter »Falsche Engel, wahre Liebe?« erhältlich ist.

📜 Beschreibung 》
Otogi Katsura gilt als Inbegriff einer liebenswerten Musterschülerin, doch ihr wahres Temperament ist deutlich schärfer, als ihre Mitschüler ahnen. Als sie am Bahnhof ein paar aufdringliche Jungs zurechtweist, wird ausgerechnet Toki Ninomae Zeuge davon. Der sonst so ruhige und perfekte Vizepräsident findet ihre ungefilterte Art überraschend reizvoll. Schon bald teilen die beiden ein Geheimnis, das ihr sauberes Schulimage gefährden könnte und sie einander gefährlich näherbringt.

images.alt:
»Hana Ichise« von Akari Kitō
Eine junge Frau aus der Ichise-Familie, einer Nebenlinie von Magiern, die das Land beschützen. Da sie ständig mit ihrer talentierten Zwillingsschwester verglichen wurde, galt sie lange als Versagerin und wurde schlecht behandelt. Eines Tages erwacht jedoch plötzlich eine gewaltige Kraft in ihr. Um frei leben zu können, hält sie diese Macht verborgen, doch als Saku ihr Geheimnis erkennt, gerät ihr ruhiges Leben völlig aus den Fugen.
»Saku Ichinomiya« von Kikunosuke Toya
Der junge Familienoberhaupt der Ichinomiya-Familie, einer der Hauptlinien von Magiern, die das Land beschützen. Er wirkt arrogant und herrisch, besitzt aber unbestreitbare Fähigkeiten. Nachdem er Hanas wahre Kraft erkennt, drängt er sie zu einer vertraglichen Ehe.

Der Anime entsteht bei TROYCA unter der Regie von Tōru Kitahata (Hinako Note, Haganai NEXT). Die Serienkomposition und das Drehbuch übernimmt Yūko Kakihara (Cells at Work!, Buddy Daddies), während das Charakterdesign von Minami Murayama stammt. Die Musik komponieren Yukari Hashimoto (Komi Can’t Communicate, March Comes in Like a Lion), Ruka Kawada und R・O・N.

📜 Beschreibung 》
Seit Jahrtausenden wird Japan von fünf Clans beschützt, die mächtige Barrieren erschaffen können. Die achtzehnjährige Hana stammt aus einer Nebenfamilie eines dieser Clans, steht jedoch seit jeher im Schatten ihrer schönen, beliebten und begabten Zwillingsschwester. Als in ihr plötzlich eine außergewöhnliche Kraft erwacht, beschließt sie, diese geheim zu halten, um ihr ruhiges Leben nicht zu verlieren. Doch als Saku Ichinomiya neuer Oberhaupt des Ichinomiya-Clans wird und nach einer starken Braut sucht, rückt Hana gefährlich nah ins Zentrum seiner Aufmerksamkeit.

Getting an AI demo to work is one thing. Getting it to work inside a real company with 15 years of workflows, systems that don't talk to each other, and a bunch of undocumented processes is a very different challenge.

A forward-deployed engineer sums it up: "The model is usually the cleanest part."
